Registry / Terms / significant durability error

significant durability error

—Not in VIM/VIML G 18 #00984 · 9 instances

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH No authoritative definition in VIM or VIML.
  2. MEDIUM 8 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

Ed.YearPublicationClauseDefinitionConsistency
20102006OIML R 76-1:2006
#00984
T.5.5.8
- - - durability error greater than e. (1) A durability error can be due to errors, occurring after a period of instrument use, are not considered to be significant durability errors, even when they exceed e, if they are clearly the result of the failure of a device/component, or of a disturbance and for which the indication: cannot be interpreted, memorized, or transmitted as a measurement result; implies the impossibility to perform any measurement; or is so obviously wrong that it is bound to be noticed by all those interested in the result of measurement.
pending
20102004OIML D 11:2004
#00235
3.12
durability error greater than the value specified in the relevant The relevant Recommendation may specify Recommendation
pending
20102004OIML R 135:2004
#01873
2.34
durability error greater than the value specified in the appropriate Recommendation
pending
20101993OIML R 105:1993
#01237
T.22
for masses equal to or greater than the minimum measured quantity, a durability error greater than one-fifth of the absolute value of the maximum permissible error for the measured quantity. Durability errors are not considered as significant when: – the indication cannot be interpreted, memorized or transmitted as a measurement result, – the indication is such that it is impossible to perform any measurement
pending
202X2022OIML R139-1:2022
#03497
3.1.15
durability error exceeding the value specified in this Recommendation [OIML V 1:2013, 5.17]
pending
202X2021OIML R060-1:2021
#03498
3.7.16
durability error exceeding the value specified in the applicable Recommendation [VIML 5.17]
pending
202X2013OIML D011:2013
#02228-D011
3.14
durability error exceeding the value specified in the applicable Recommendation [VIML 5.17]
pending
202X2006OIML R 76-1:2006
#00984
T.5.5.8
durability error greater than e. errors, occurring after a period of instrument use, are not considered to be significant durability errors, even when they exceed e, if they are clearly the result of the failure of a device/component, or of a disturbance and for which the indication: cannot be interpreted, memorized, or transmitted as a measurement result; implies the impossibility to perform any measurement; or is so obviously wrong that it is bound to be noticed by all those interested in the result of measurement.

durability error greater than the value specified in the appropriate Recommendation [OIML D 11, 3.12]
